steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts 'many ______ studies' with the researchers' 'novel path.' The word 'novel' means new and different, so it only creates a contrast if the prior studies represent the standard approach. The blank must describe what the typical, mainstream studies look like.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — eccentric: Means bizarrely unconventional. If prior studies were already eccentric, there would be no contrast with a 'novel path' — both would be unusual. ✗
  • Choice B — meager: Means small in quantity. The blank describes the type of prior approach, not how many studies exist. The passage says nothing about the volume of prior research. ✗
  • Choice C — conventional: Means following the standard, accepted approach. 'Many conventional studies focus on people above sea level' sets up a clear contrast with the novel choice to study people below sea level. ✓
  • Choice D — random: Means without plan or pattern. The prior studies were systematic — they consistently focused on people above sea level — so calling them random contradicts the passage. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
